# charts

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Organisational parent for the two Helm charts (benthos-collector, openmeter) that package all OpenMeter Kubernetes workloads. Owns the shared helm-docs generation pipeline (Makefile + template.md) consumed by both child charts.

## Patterns

**helm-docs via shared template** — READMEs for both child charts are generated by `helm-docs` using the shared template.md defined at this level. Run `make docs` to regenerate. Never hand-edit a chart README.md. (`make docs  # invokes helm-docs with template.md for benthos-collector and openmeter`)
**OCI Helm registry distribution** — Charts are packaged with `helm package deploy/charts/<CHART> --version <VERSION> --destination build/helm` and pushed to ghcr.io/openmeterio/helm-charts/. Chart.yaml version and appVersion must stay aligned with the container image tag. (`helm package deploy/charts/openmeter --version 1.2.3 --destination build/helm`)
**Self-contained child charts** — Each child chart (benthos-collector, openmeter) must be fully self-contained. No shared Kubernetes manifests, CRDs, or cluster-scoped RBAC live at the deploy/charts level. (`# Each chart has its own templates/, values.yaml, Chart.yaml, and README.tmpl.md`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `deploy/charts/Makefile` | Runs helm-docs across both child charts using the shared template.md. The sole command is `make docs`. | helm-docs must be available (it is in the Nix CI shell). Running outside Nix silently produces no output if helm-docs is missing. |
| `deploy/charts/template.md` | Shared Go-template for helm-docs defining badge helpers (versionBadge, typeBadge, appVersionBadge, artifactHubBadge) and the tldr / chart.base layout consumed by both child README templates. | Editing badge or section templates here affects both child chart READMEs. The artifactHubBadge URL is derived from `.Name`, so chart names must not change without updating ArtifactHub listings. |
| `deploy/charts/benthos-collector/templates/_helpers.tpl` | Defines all named template helpers for the benthos-collector chart: fullname, componentName, labels, selectorLabels, benthos-collector.args. | All resource names and args must route through these helpers — never hardcode release or chart name strings directly in resource files. |
| `deploy/charts/openmeter/templates/_helpers.tpl` | Defines all named template helpers for the openmeter chart: componentName, fullname, componentLabels, componentSelectorLabels. | All app.kubernetes.io/* labels and resource names must be generated through these helpers — inline label sets will diverge from selector labels causing broken Services. |
| `deploy/charts/openmeter/templates/configmap.yaml` | Single shared ConfigMap mounted as /etc/openmeter/config.yaml across all OpenMeter binaries. Optional infrastructure blocks are guarded by enabled flags. | Every optional infrastructure section (Kafka, ClickHouse, Redis, Svix) must have a matching `{{- if .Values.<service>.enabled -}}` guard here AND in the corresponding resource template. |
| `deploy/charts/openmeter/values.yaml` | Single source of defaults for all OpenMeter workloads. All OpenMeter binaries share Values.image.repository and Values.image.tag — only Svix uses its own image fields. | Never add per-binary image fields — version skew across mono-repo binaries is prevented by sharing a single image tag. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments; edits will be overwritten on next `make docs`
- Adding a third chart at this level without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and updating the Makefile docs target
- Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at deploy/charts level — each chart must be fully self-contained
- Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag used in values.yaml
- Adding optional infrastructure to the openmeter chart without both an enabled guard in the resource template AND a corresponding override block in configmap.yaml

## Decisions

- **Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart** — Both charts share the same badge set and TL;DR install pattern; centralising the template ensures consistent branding and eliminates duplication across helm-docs runs.
- **OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index** — OCI registries are supported natively by Helm 3+ and integrate with the same GHCR credentials used for container images, simplifying the release pipeline.
- **All OpenMeter binaries share one image and tag in the openmeter chart** — The mono-repo produces all binaries from a single Dockerfile and release tag; sharing one image tag enforces version consistency and prevents accidental partial upgrades.

## Example: Adding a new OpenMeter workload to the openmeter chart

```
# 1. Add a new Deployment template (e.g. templates/my-worker.yaml) using helpers:
#    {{ include "openmeter.componentName" (list . "my-worker") }}
#    labels: {{ include "openmeter.componentLabels" (list . "my-worker") | nindent 4 }}
#    Add checksum/config annotation on pod template:
#    checksum/config: {{ include (print $.Template.BasePath "/configmap.yaml") . | sha256sum }}
# 2. Add enabled guard in values.yaml: myWorker.enabled: true
# 3. Wrap the template: {{- if .Values.myWorker.enabled }}
# 4. Reuse Values.image.repository and Values.image.tag — never add a separate image field.
# 5. Run: helm lint deploy/charts/openmeter
```

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
